content.problemStatement:
Let's try to solve the Fractional Knapsack Problem.

You have a knapsack that can take upto WmaxW_{max}Wmax‚Äã weight. 

Given NNN items, ithi^{th}ith of them having the weight WiW_iWi‚Äã and value ViV_iVi‚Äã. You can pick some or all the items completely or fractionally so that their weight does not exceed WmaxW_{max}Wmax‚Äã. 

Your task is to maximise the total value of the items you will carry in your bag.

**Note -** If you take ab\frac{a}{b}ba‚Äã fraction of an item (b>a)(b > a)(b>a), it's weight will be aWib\frac{a W_i}{b}baWi‚Äã‚Äã and it's value will be aVib\frac{a V_i}{b}baVi‚Äã‚Äã.

Print the maximum value with relative error upto 10‚àí210^{-2}10‚àí2.


Input Format

The first line contains a two integer NNN and WmaxW_{max}Wmax‚Äã, denoting the length of array AAA and the maximum weight you can carry.
Next NNN lines contains the description of the items.

ithi^{th}ith line contains two integers WiW_iWi‚Äã and ViV_iVi‚Äã, denoting the Weight and Value of the ithi^{th}ith item




Output Format
Output the maximum value you can carry while not exceeding the weight limit of the knapsack.

solution.approach:
Sort items by **value-to-weight ratio**.
Pick items greedily until the knapsack is full.
Take fractions only when the remaining capacity is insufficient.
This approach is:


Correct
Efficient
Optimal
